steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what "constantly" most nearly means as used in the passage. Alexander is described as taking a desk at an engineering firm and being at work almost constantly — the context emphasizes how fully he committed to work, avoiding clubs and dining alone.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Nonstop': Fits perfectly. Working "almost nonstop" captures the relentless, continuous effort the passage conveys. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Quickly': Speed is not implied; the passage focuses on duration and dedication, not pace. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Fearfully': No fear or anxiety is suggested; the tone is one of focused industry. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Enough': "Enough" describes sufficiency, not continuity; it does not fit the sense of unbroken effort. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
